text: Ama basa na be kechena anyi be kasawule ere so beeŇč shi kanan…õ anyeeŇč baa shuli a ku atutu fan…õ bumo nna.
translated: Ama spoke in the Kechena language to them, and Kasawule said there, "Thus, he will know that they will do well to come and greet my children."

ref: The Lord God said, ‚ÄúIt isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.‚ÄĚ
text: Kede be kaman n…õ Eny…õnpe Eb…Ēr…õ kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúK maŇč daga fan…õ eny…õn na nawule e baa w…Ēt…Ē. MeeŇč p…Ēr ech…õtopo n…õ e beeŇč kukwe mo n sa mo.‚ÄĚ
translated: ‚ÄúI have spoken the word that God gave me to the people there. Those who are in the house of the one whose name is there have done the work.‚ÄĚ

ref: But the servant asked, ‚ÄúWhat if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?‚ÄĚ
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Eebraham be kay…õrbi na bishi mo ny…õnpe le: ‚ÄúTo, n…õ k kaŇč ba fan…õ eche na maa sha ketu ma m ba efuli ere so nfe, kumo ere Ňč keta fo kebia na n y…Ē fo efuli so ndoŇč n ya ta eche na n sa mo a?‚ÄĚ
translated: Then Abraham spoke to them, saying, ‚ÄúLord, if there are fifty righteous people in the city, will you indeed destroy it? Would you not spare the place for the sake of the fifty righteous who are in it?‚ÄĚ

ref: But they replied, ‚ÄúSir, why do you say such things? We would never do anything like that!
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ b bishi mo le: ‚ÄúMan…õ e ba n…õ fo, an ny…õnpe bee malga le ere? Kaar…õ gba n…õ anyi, fo anya e wora loŇč!
translated: The father and the child said: "They said he did it with what? Why are you questioning them here? Take it with you, then he will give it to you!"

ref: and I'm going to find out the truth. I swear by the life of the king that you won't leave this place until your youngest brother comes here.
text: Kanan…õ anyi al…õ beeŇč wora m pin fan…õ kashenteŇč n…õ menyee ji nde: M b…Ē ewura F…õ…õro be kuwurputi n…õ e tase so ere fan…õ men maaŇč lar nfe ama men be kaluge na baŇč ba nfe nna p…Ē…õŇč.
translated: Moses said to the people of Israel: Pharaoh will drive you out of this land, and when he does, you will not leave empty-handed, but each woman will take from her neighbor silver and gold jewelry and clothing, and you will put them on your children.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Kumo be kaman n…õ e kule Eb…Ēr…õ Ňč kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúO, Eny…õnpe, n ny…õnpe Eebraham be Eb…Ēr…õ, sa ma kumulela kabre n wora n ny…õnpe Eebraham kelela.
translated: Then the elder said to his Ebori: "O, Eny…õnpe, I am of Abraham in Ebori, so that my descendants will also be of Abraham there."

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Amoso mee kule fo nna fan…õ fo m…Ēlga ma ashi n da Is…Ē…Ē be en…Ē to, Ňčkpal man…õ so, mee l…Ē kufu nna fan…õ e bee ba nna n…õ e ba k…Ē ma n…õ ma beche n…õ mbia kena.
translated: Amonso, my child, listen to your father's word, for on the day you came with Is…Ē…Ē, they taught you. Follow them, thus you will not die; the word they give you, they will take you to live in the land.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: LoŇč be eboŇč ana na be kumo n…õ k juŇčkpar be ketre e daa la Pishon. K daa shile nna a kulti Havila be efuli.
translated: The name of the first is the Pishon; it winds through the entire land of Havilah where there is gold. The gold of that land is good.
